content.explanation:
🏗️ The Real Structure of Every Website

Every website is built inside a strict structure that tells the browser how to read your page.

Without this structure, browsers may still try to guess your content — but the result can break on different devices or behave unpredictably.

🧠 Think of it like a Human Body

  • 🧬 <html> = the full body
  • 🧠 <head> = the brain (settings, hidden info)
  • 👀 <body> = everything visible (what users see)
content.syntax_breakdown:
🧪 The Standard HTML Blueprint

Every professional webpage follows this exact structure:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
  <head>
    <title>My Website</title>
  </head>

  <body>
    <h1>Hello World</h1>
  </body>
</html>

🎯 Key Idea

Everything visible MUST go inside the <body> tag.

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
Structural Blueprint Active

Everything inside this body block renders directly onto the screen.

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Modify your webpage structure

1. Add a new <h2> tag inside the <body>
2. The text must be: Valid Architecture Layer
3. Place it BELOW the paragraph

👉 Then run your code and see how structure affects layout
mini_challenge.hint:
👉 Insert this BEFORE :

Valid Architecture Layer

Make sure it is inside the body section.

quiz_engine.question: Where does all visible content (text, images, buttons) go in an HTML page?
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 The <body> tag contains everything users see on the webpage, while <head> stores hidden settings and metadata.

takeaways: Every HTML page must follow a proper structure

cheat_sheet.doctype: Tells browser to use modern HTML standards
cheat_sheet.html: Root container of the entire webpage
cheat_sheet.head: Stores metadata and page settings
